reviews.title: Disc 1 Memories
reviews.body: When my roommate and best fried in college started getting into The Dead, he played Disc 1 of this show a lot. I remember riding in his Jeep and listening to Loser. That tune got me into The Dead, and to this day I think it is the best version of any song Ive heard them do (though there are a number of close seconds). Other memorable tunes that come to mind from Disc 1 are Browned Eyed Women, They Love Each Other, and El Paso. What an impact this music had on us! The funny thing is, someone had left Disc 1 with him on accident once, and it was the only part of the show we knew. It was all scratched up because he always kept it in his Jeep. Disc 1 alone stood the test of our college career as the best Dead we ever heard/acquired. I remember him saying Man, I wonder if the rest of the show is this good? Well it is! The Dancin In The Streets is almost not human, and the Scarlet-Fire is killer. Can you imagine if we had had Disc 2 all that time! Hopefully my roommate will get around to reading the email I sent him that points him to this site, and download the rest of the show (plus, he deserves a fresh copy of Disc 1). What a great website this is!!!

reviews.title: Golden Goose
reviews.body: Ok now that it's stream-able only, we need GD Merchandising to move this show to Dick's Picks. I'll buy it. Add the info on the show (poster, ticket, t-shirt) and the GD entourage can continue to get paid. Just like the old days. Watching Jerry huff and puff up the stairs and forgetfully nodding and noodling during the entire Tour From Hell including RFK and Soldier Field '95, you would think that someone (please!) in the band or stage crew or family would have cared enough to said, Jerry, WE are done. Lets get you well. Now I love those cowboys, I love their gold I love my uncle, God rest his soul Taught me good, Lord, taught me all I know Taught me so well, I grabbed that gold And I left his dead ass there by the side of the road. Oh yea, that Dancin Jam is the best.

reviews.title: fantastic show
reviews.body: how can one write about cornell intelligently? it has been hyped since (at least) my mid-80s college years. what will one find here? perfection. what will they not find? flawed passion. yes, flawed passion. as one who got into the dead in the 80s, i was always attracted to the poorly recorded, messy but antagonistic recordings of the sixties. what we find here in cornell is the rise of professionalism meeting passion. yes, it is an amazingly well-played, passionate show. however, it is professionalism. ironically, later shows began to disintegrate back into garage rock flaws, mishaps, ripping feedback and 80s heads mythologize cornell rather than 9-19-90, a show (imho) far more exploratory, innovative, quizzical and loose then 5-8-77 will ever be. it's amazing to me. after witnessing one of the longest dead shows in history (6-20?-94) in autzen stadium, all people could do was bitch. what makes cornell unique is that it was part of a run when the dead played short, concentrated shows. the second set is little over an hour! but that short set meant business. is it the best morning dew ever? hardly. there are other greats, commendable for attributes that did not exist during the spring of '77. don't get me wrong, i'm a fan. but check out binghampton '77; check out the egypt return in '78 at the winterland; 7-7/8-78; the morning dew from saratoga in '83; everything leading to the morning dew from augusta in '84. cornell is known for perfection. i'll grant it that. in it's brevity, it misses the nuance of more messed up, space/angst-ridden shows in the '80s, like the '84 greeks or new years '81. all i'm saying is that this is good, but there ain't no holy grail. shucks, it ain't the dew but that scarlet->fire transition that smokes. what about binghampton? normal? san fran? with these e-tree benefactors and the archive portal, we are a lucky audience. dig cornell, but don't forget, that ain't it. 10-14-83 (how about that scarlet->fire? damn). msg '83? forget about it. out

reviews.title: My Best Show from a Harley Deadhead
reviews.body: I have seen (some times) and heard (some times) over 35 shows, which is not a lot compared to most Deadheads. However, they are my favorite band and I love and miss Jerry very much. I am now what you might call a Yuppy, since I am over 40 years old and ride Harley Davidson Motorcycles, but this show goes every where I go. This show has made a 4,200 mile trip from Atlanta, GA to Sturgis, SD for the 2004 Rally, with this show playing most of the way. This is the best sounding and one of the best performances I have heard the Grateful Dead perform. It is my favorite show of all times. Please give it a listen, I know that you will enjoy it. My favorite it the Scarlet Fire or the Jack Straw. I dedicate this to my Friend in Lyons, Co who introduced me to the Grateful Dead some twenty years ago. We have shared some great shows together and some great trout fishing.

reviews.title: Keith sucks!
reviews.body: OK yeah this one is for the agesno question...but if there is any question why Keith was sacked just listen to the Scarlet into Fire jam. Manomanachevits does he suck! His incessant "premature fire overlay" is such a fucking drag when Jerry, Bob and Phil are galloping through the cosmos where the only foot hold available is planet to planet! If you can get Keith drool out of your ears then this is a remarkable show. Anyway, just me being a spoiled brat again! Sorry
